title: Response Commitment, Problem Details, Streaming, and Downloads · Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A production review compares near-miss TypeScript decisions for response commitment, problem details, streaming, and downloads and demands the smallest safe single-owner response and bounded stream protocol with changed-input evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for single-owner response and bounded stream protocol. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose status and fields before commitment, assign one response owner, return after terminal sends, and observe writableEnded, headersSent, finish, close, and error distinctly.
Map stable internal failures to RFC 9457 types, status, title, detail, instance, extensions, localization, and disclosure policy.
Control JSON nullability, undefined omission, BigInt, dates, non-finite numbers, property order, cycles, and serialization failure before commitment.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
